records.description: A child hood experiece  seems more real like an alien encounter then the "dream" I thought it was
Battle of LA: 1,400+ AA rounds fired at objects. Searchlights converged. Five civilians died.
6 doughnut-shaped objects over Puget Sound. One dropped molten slag onto boat. Army CIC investigated.
Pilot Kenneth Arnold: 9 crescent objects at 1,200mph. Coined 'flying saucer.' First modern UFO sighting.
Army announced 'flying disc' recovery, retracted to 'weather balloon.' Later attributed to Project Mogul.

PURSUE included the FBI Dallas field-office memo to HQ reporting that an Air Force major said 'an object purporting to be a flying disc was recovered near Roswell.' Memo: 'hexagonal in shape, suspended from a balloon ~20 ft in diameter.'
FBI Dallas memo to HQ: an Air Force major reported a recovered 'flying disc' near Roswell, 'hexagonal in shape, suspended from a balloon ~20 ft in diameter.' Later included in PURSUE.
Capt. Mantell crashed P-51 pursuing massive bright object. First pilot fatality linked to UFO.
The most-viewed FBI Vault file. Agent Guy Hottel relayed an informant claim of three 'flying saucers' recovered in New Mexico, each ~50 ft, with occupants. Never substantiated by the FBI.

Objects on radar at National Airport + Andrews AFB. F-94s scrambled. Repeated Jul 26. Largest Pentagon press conf since WWII.
